```text
     1→/**
     2→ * APOD Proxy — Cloudflare Worker
     3→ *
     4→ * Serves NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day from a stable URL.
     5→ * Uses Cloudflare's edge cache for both the API response and the
     6→ * image bytes — no KV, no cron, no external storage.
     7→ *
     8→ * Routes:
     9→ *   GET /      → today's APOD image (high res, falls back to standard)
    10→ *   GET /sd    → today's APOD image (standard res)
    11→ *   GET /info  → JSON metadata
    12→ *   GET /about → plain-text description and attribution
    13→ */
    14→
    15→export interface Env {
    16→  NASA_API_KEY?: string;
    17→}
    18→
    19→const NASA_APOD_URL = "https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod";
    20→const MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS = 7;
    21→const CACHE_TTL = 3600;
    22→
    23→interface ApodResponse {
    24→  date: string;
    25→  title: string;
    26→  explanation: string;
    27→  url: string;
    28→  hdurl?: string;
    29→  media_type: "image" | "video";
    30→  copyright?: string;
    31→}
    32→
    33→function formatDate(d: Date): string {
    34→  return d.toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    35→}
    36→
    37→class NasaApiError extends Error {
    38→  constructor(public statusCode: number, message: string) {
    39→    super(message);
    40→  }
    41→}
    42→
    43→async function fetchApod(date: string, apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    44→  const url = `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}&date=${date}`;
    45→
    46→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    47→    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
    48→  });
    49→
    50→  if (!res.ok) {
    51→    throw new NasaApiError(res.status,
    52→      res.status === 429
    53→        ? "NASA API rate limit exceeded — try again later"
    54→        : `NASA API returned ${res.status}`
    55→    );
    56→  }
    57→
    58→  return res.json() as Promise<ApodResponse>;
    59→}
    60→
    61→let cachedImage: { date: string; apod: ApodResponse } | null = null;
    62→
    63→async function findLatestImage(apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    64→  const today = formatDate(new Date());
    65→
    66→  if (cachedImage && cachedImage.date === today) {
    67→    return cachedImage.apod;
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  let videoStreak = 0;
    71→
    72→  for (let i = 0; i <= MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS; i++) {
    73→    const d = new Date();
    74→    d.setUTCDate(d.getUTCDate() - i);
    75→
    76→    const apod = await fetchApod(formatDate(d), apiKey);
    77→    if (apod.media_type === "image") {
    78→      cachedImage = { date: today, apod };
    79→      return apod;
    80→    }
    81→    videoStreak++;
    82→  }
    83→
    84→  throw new Error(
    85→    `Today's APOD is a video, and the last ${videoStreak} days have been too — no image available`
    86→  );
    87→}
    88→
    89→async function fetchImageCached(url: string): Promise<Response> {
    90→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    91→    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
    92→  });
    93→
    94→  if (!res.ok) {
    95→    throw new Error(`Image fetch failed: ${res.status}`);
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  return res;
    99→}
   100→
   101→function baseHeaders(origin: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, string> {
   102→  return {
   103→    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   104→    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
   105→    "Link": [
   106→      `<${origin}/>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="HD image"`,
   107→      `<${origin}/sd>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="SD image"`,
   108→      `<${origin}/info>; rel="describedby"`,
   109→      `<${origin}/about>; rel="about"`,
   110→    ].join(", "),
   111→    ...headers,
   112→  };
   113→}
   114→
   115→export default {
   116→  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
   117→    if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
   118→      return new Response(null, {
   119→        headers: {
   120→          "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   121→          "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, OPTIONS",
   122→          "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
   123→        },
   124→      });
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    const reqUrl = new URL(request.url);
   128→    const { pathname } = reqUrl;
   129→    const origin = reqUrl.origin;
   130→
   131→    try {
   132→      const apiKey = env.NASA_API_KEY || "DEMO_KEY";
   133→
   134→      if (pathname === "/" || pathname === "/sd") {
   135→        const apod = await findLatestImage(apiKey);
   136→        const imageUrl = pathname === "/sd"
   137→          ? apod.url
   138→          : (apod.hdurl || apod.url);
   139→
   140→        const upstream = await fetchImageCached(imageUrl);
   141→        const contentType = upstream.headers.get("Content-Type") || "image/jpeg";
   142→
   143→        return new Response(upstream.body, {
   144→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": contentType }),
   145→        });
   146→      }
   147→
   148→      if (pathname === "/info") {
   149→        const endpoints = {
   150→          "/": "Today's APOD image (high resolution, falls back to standard)",
   151→          "/sd": "Today's APOD image (standard resolution)",
   152→          "/info": "JSON metadata for today's APOD",
   153→          "/about": "Plain-text description and attribution",
   154→        };
   155→
   156→        try {
   157→          const apod = await fetchApod(formatDate(new Date()), apiKey);
   158→
   159→          return new Response(
   160→            JSON.stringify({
   161→              title: apod.title,
   162→              date: apod.date,
   163→              explanation: apod.explanation,
   164→              media_type: apod.media_type,
   165→              url: apod.url,
   166→              hdurl: apod.hdurl ?? null,
   167→              copyright: apod.copyright ?? null,
   168→              endpoints,
   169→            }, null, 2),
   170→            { headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   171→          );
   172→        } catch (err) {
   173→          const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   174→          const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
   175→          return new Response(
   176→            JSON.stringify({ error: message, endpoints }, null, 2),
   177→            { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   178→          );
   179→        }
   180→      }
   181→
   182→      if (pathname === "/about") {
   183→        const text = [
   184→          "",
   185→          "     .        *          .          *          .        *",
   186→          "   .         *                        .          .",
   187→          "                 _   _    _    ____    _",
   188→          "     *          | \\ | |  / \\  / ___|  / \\         .",
   189→          "        .       |  \\| | / _ \\ \\___ \\ / _ \\",
   190→          "           .    | |\\  |/ ___ \\ ___) / ___ \\   *",
   191→          "     .          |_| \\_/_/   \\_\\____/_/   \\_\\        .",
   192→          "  *                              .                *",
   193→          "        .    *        .    *          .        .",
   194→          "",
   195→          "                   ── APOD Proxy ──",
   196→          "",
   197→          "  Proxy for NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day.",
   198→          "  Returns today's image at a static URL, no API key required.",
   199→          "  Video days are skipped automatically.",
   200→          "  Useful for wallpapers, <img> sources, and other zero-JS environments.",
   201→          "",
   202→          "  Usage:",
   203→          `    <img src="${origin}/">`,
   204→          "",
   205→          "  Routes:",
   206→          "    /        HD image (default)",
   207→          "    /sd      Standard resolution",
   208→          "    /info    JSON metadata",
   209→          "    /about   This page",
   210→          "",
   211→          "  Built by Grant Keller — https://grantkeller.dev",
   212→          "",
   213→        ].join("\n");
   214→
   215→        return new Response(text, {

   219→
   220→      return new Response(
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 22:58:55Z)**_

The NASA API with `DEMO_KEY` returns 200 right now. A few questions:

1. **Where are you seeing the error?** — locally, or on the deployed worker at the Cloudflare URL?
2. **Which route?** — `/`, `/sd`, `/info`?

If it's on the deployed worker, the issue might be that `NASA_API_KEY` isn't set as a Wrangler secret (so it falls back to `DEMO_KEY`), and the deployed worker's IP has been rate-limited by NASA. The Cloudflare edge cache should prevent most repeat calls, but if the cache expired and multiple requests hit simultaneously, it could exhaust the DEMO_KEY limit (30/hr, 50/day).

The fix would be to set a real NASA API key (1,000 req/hr):

```bash
npx wrangler secret put NASA_API_KEY
```

Do you have a NASA API key set up, or do you need to register for one at [api.nasa.gov](https://api.nasa.gov)?

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 22:59:30Z)**_

Interesting — the NASA API works fine when I curl it directly, but the local worker gets a 429. This is likely **Wrangler's local dev server caching the 429 response**. The `cf: { cacheTtl, cacheEverything }` options tell Cloudflare's edge cache to cache the response, and Wrangler's local simulator may have cached that original 429 and keeps serving it.

Try restarting the dev server — that should clear the local cache:

```bash
# Kill and restart
npm run dev
```

If the 429 persists after a restart, we can add a bypass so rate limit errors aren't cached. Want me to add that?

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:01:35Z)**_

The direct API call works fine with `DEMO_KEY`. The issue is that **Wrangler's local dev uses `workerd` which has its own fetch behavior** — it might be getting rate-limited differently, or there could be a cached 429 in Wrangler's local persistence.

Let me check for Wrangler's local cache/state files:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:01:48Z)**_

No cached state files. The `cf: { cacheTtl, cacheEverything }` options are **ignored in local dev** — Wrangler doesn't simulate the Cloudflare cache. So it's hitting NASA's API every time.

The `DEMO_KEY` limit is **30 requests/hour and 50/day**. With lookback logic, each page load could burn up to 8 requests. You likely exhausted the daily limit yesterday, and `DEMO_KEY` limits might be per-IP with a longer cooldown than expected.

The simplest fix — create a `.dev.vars` with a real API key:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:02:31Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Check rate limit headers from direct call

`curl -s "https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod?api_key=DEMO_KEY&date=2026-03-26" -D - 2>&1 | grep -i "x-ratelimit"`

```text
access-control-expose-headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining
x-ratelimit-limit: 10
x-ratelimit-remaining: 7
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:02:38Z)**_

Right — good catch, the direct curl works fine. So the problem is specifically how `workerd` (Wrangler's local runtime) makes the request. It might be using a different IP path, or more likely — **the 429 response got cached by `workerd`'s local fetch implementation** despite there being no explicit cache state files.

You also asked to make sure 429s don't get cached. Let me fix that — we should skip the cache options when the response is a rate limit error, or better yet, not cache error responses at all.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:02:39Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./src/index.ts`

```text
    43→async function fetchApod(date: string, apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    44→  const url = `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}&date=${date}`;
    45→
    46→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    47→    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
    48→  });
    49→
    50→  if (!res.ok) {
    51→    throw new NasaApiError(res.status,
    52→      res.status === 429
    53→        ? "NASA API rate limit exceeded — try again later"
    54→        : `NASA API returned ${res.status}`
    55→    );
    56→  }
    57→
    58→  return res.json() as Promise<ApodResponse>;
    59→}
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:02:47Z)**_

The issue: `cf: { cacheTtl, cacheEverything }` tells Cloudflare (and `workerd` locally) to cache **all responses** including 429s. A cached 429 would persist for the full `CACHE_TTL` (3600s = 1 hour). But you said it's been 10 hours — so either the cache renewed itself on repeated hits, or `workerd` persists it differently.

The fix is to make the initial fetch without cache directives, check the status, then re-fetch with caching only on success — or simpler, use `cacheTtl` with `cacheTtlByStatus` to explicitly not cache errors:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:02:49Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

```text
<tool_use_error>Found 2 matches of the string to replace, but replace_all is false. To replace all occurrences, set replace_all to true. To replace only one occurrence, please provide more context to uniquely identify the instance.
String:   const res = await fetch(url, {
    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
  });</tool_use_error>
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:02:54Z)**_

Both `fetchApod` and `fetchImageCached` use the same pattern. Let me update both:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:03:36Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:03:37Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Type-check the changes

```text
(Bash completed with no output)
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:03:42Z)**_

Type-checks clean. Now restart your dev server and try again — `cacheTtlByStatus` with `-1` for error codes tells Cloudflare (and `workerd`) to **never cache** non-2xx responses, so a 429 won't get stuck.

The root cause: the original `cacheTtl` cached everything including the 429, and `workerd` kept serving it. With `cacheTtlByStatus`, only successful responses get cached.

_**User (2026-03-26 23:18:30Z)**_

[fetchApod] status=429 url=https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod?api_key=DEMO_KEY&date=2026-03-26 body={
  "error": {
    "code": "OVER_RATE_LIMIT",
    "message": "You have exceeded your rate limit. Try again later or contact us at https://api.nasa.gov:443/contact/ for assistance"
  }
}

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:18:39Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:18:43Z)**_

So it's a real 429 — NASA is rate-limiting `DEMO_KEY` from `workerd`'s requests (probably different request fingerprint than your terminal curl). The `DEMO_KEY` limit is only 30 req/hr / 50 req/day.

The real fix: get a free NASA API key (1,000 req/hr). It's instant, no approval needed.

1. Go to https://api.nasa.gov — fill in name/email, you'll get a key immediately
2. Then:

```bash
echo 'NASA_API_KEY=your-key-here' > .dev.vars
```

That file is already gitignored. Want me to remove the debug logging now, or leave it until we confirm the key works?

---

_**User (2026-03-26 23:24:35Z)**_

is a NASA API key tied to a specific email address? should I create a project-specific gmail just for thi?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 23:24:40Z)**_

No, don't bother — just use your personal email. NASA API keys are free, unlimited registrations, and there's no billing or sensitive access tied to them. It's just for rate limit tracking. You can always generate another one later if needed.
